name: D. Nathan Sheets
bio: D. Nathan Sheets served as Under Secretary of the Treasury for International Affairs from 2014 to 2017 and is now Global Chief Economist at Citigroup. He previously spent 18 years at the Federal Reserve, including as Director of the Division of International Finance.

stances.position_label: Market-oriented with government role in stability and level playing field
stances.summary: Sheets emphasized structural flexibility, clear property rights, and market openness as distinguishing features of the U.S. economy, while supporting government intervention to ensure financial stability and a level playing field. He advocated for financial sector reforms post-crisis and structural reforms including business tax reform, but consistently stressed market-oriented solutions with government ensuring rules-based frameworks.

sources.title: Remarks by Under Secretary for International Affairs Nathan Sheets at The Peterson Institute for International Economics on The Global Economy
sources.quote: Historically, the U.S. economy has been distinguished by structural flexibility in its labor and product markets, clear property rights, commitment to the rule of law, and openness to foreign trade and investment.

sources.title: Remarks by Under Secretary Nathan Sheets at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace
sources.quote: Fundamentally, we aim to foster strong, sustainable, balanced, and inclusive global growth. This requires promoting economic resilience, openness, and a level global playing field for our workers and firms.

stances.position_label: Pro-free trade with rules-based framework; opposes unilateral protectionism
stances.summary: Sheets strongly supported expanding free trade through high-standard agreements like the Trans-Pacific Partnership and worked to reduce trade-distorting government export financing. He opposed unilateral tariffs or trade barriers, instead advocating for multilateral frameworks to bring all major economies into common rules, including China.

sources.title: Remarks by Under Secretary for International Affairs Nathan Sheets at The Peterson Institute for International Economics on The Global Economy
sources.quote: We are also actively engaged in pursuing the Administration's trade agenda through high-standard agreements such as the Trans-Pacific Partnership, which will expand opportunities and drive further integration with key trading partners.

sources.title: Testimony of Under Secretary Nathan Sheets Before the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform
sources.quote: Openness means that goods and capital are free to flow across borders to their most productive uses.

sources.title: NATHAN SHEETS Undersecretary for International Affairs U.S. Department of the Treasury - Brookings Institution Transcript
sources.quote: to open markets by liberalizing China's investment rules, including through negotiations on mutually beneficial high standard bilateral investment treaty

stances.position_label: Supports fiscal stimulus when conditions warrant; conditional on fiscal space
stances.summary: Sheets advocated for forceful fiscal stimulus during periods of economic weakness and high unemployment, including temporary expenditure boosts and tax cuts, but emphasized this should occur where governments have 'put its house in order and has sufficient budget space.' This suggests conditional support for deficit spending rather than unconditional expansion.

sources.title: Remarks by Under Secretary for International Affairs Nathan Sheets at The Peterson Institute for International Economics on The Global Economy
sources.quote: we at the U.S. Treasury have argued for countries to forcefully employ strong stimulative policies to address this situation. Such stimulus can include monetary measures, where inflation is well contained, and fiscal measures, where the government has put its house in order and has sufficient budget space.

sources.title: Remarks by Under Secretary for International Affairs Nathan Sheets at The Peterson Institute for International Economics on The Global Economy
sources.quote: The United States took forceful action to stimulate demand, fully employing both monetary and fiscal tools... by temporarily boosting expenditures and extending unemployment benefits, as well as through supportive tax cuts

stances.position_label: Supports labor market flexibility with demand-side support during downturns
stances.summary: Sheets advocated for labor market flexibility including allowing firms greater scope to hire, fire, and negotiate wages as part of structural reforms. However, he also supported extending unemployment benefits and demand-side stimulus to protect workers during economic weakness, suggesting a balance between flexibility and social protection.

sources.title: Remarks by Under Secretary for International Affairs Nathan Sheets at The Peterson Institute for International Economics on The Global Economy
sources.quote: Examples of such reforms include measures to make labor markets more flexible by allowing firms greater scope to hire, fire, and negotiate wages

sources.title: Remarks by Under Secretary for International Affairs Nathan Sheets at The Peterson Institute for International Economics on The Global Economy
sources.quote: The United States took forceful action to stimulate demand... extending unemployment benefits

stances.position_label: Supports low-carbon transition; mentions but does not emphasize aggressive mandates
stances.summary: Sheets mentioned 'low carbon global' economy as part of U.S.-China dialogue objectives and supported addressing excess industrial capacity (including in carbon-intensive sectors), but his public remarks focused more on economic rebalancing and market access than on aggressive emissions mandates. Evidence is limited and indirect.

sources.title: NATHAN SHEETS Undersecretary for International Affairs U.S. Department of the Treasury - Brookings Institution Transcript
NATHAN SHEETS Undersecretary for International Affairs U.S. Department of the Treasury - Brookings Institution Transcript
sources.quote: We are also encouraging the Chinese authorities to reduce excess industrial capacity

stances.position_label: Supports business tax reform; supported temporary tax cuts as stimulus
stances.summary: Sheets mentioned business tax reform as part of the Administration's structural reform agenda and supported 'supportive tax cuts' as part of crisis response. However, he did not articulate clear positions on corporate or high-earner tax rates in normal conditions, making his overall position difficult to place precisely.

sources.title: Remarks by Under Secretary for International Affairs Nathan Sheets at The Peterson Institute for International Economics on The Global Economy
sources.quote: The Administration's structural reform agenda includes reforming the business tax system

sources.title: Remarks by Under Secretary for International Affairs Nathan Sheets at The Peterson Institute for International Economics on The Global Economy

stances.position_label: Active global leadership through international institutions
stances.summary: As Treasury Under Secretary for International Affairs, Sheets consistently advocated for robust U.S. engagement in multilateral institutions including the IMF, World Bank, G-20, and regional development banks. He emphasized that these institutions are 'vital to our economic interests' and that U.S. leadership in them protects American jobs and exports while advancing global economic stability.

sources.title: Testimony of Treasury Under Secretary for International Affairs Nathan Sheets before the House Financial Services Subcommittee on Monetary Policy And Trade
sources.quote: To ensure that the IMF remains at the center of the multilateral economic system – and that we maintain an important voice in it – the United States should promptly approve the 2010 quota and governance reforms...a well-resourced and effective IMF is indispensable to achieving our economic and national security interests.

sources.title: Remarks by Under Secretary Nathan Sheets at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace
sources.quote: Fourth, we engage multilaterally to advance U.S. perspectives at forums such as the G-7 and G-20, and through institutions like the IMF and the multilateral development banks. We also play a leadership role in keeping these organizations effective, relevant to the global economy, and reflective of U.S. values and interests.

stances.position_label: Supports maintaining defense and security-related international commitments
stances.summary: While not directly addressing defense spending levels, Sheets emphasized U.S. leadership in international security and economic stability. His testimony highlighted support for IMF involvement in Greece's debt crisis and other stabilization efforts, suggesting a preference for maintaining international commitments rather than retrenchment.

sources.title: Testimony of Treasury Under Secretary for International Affairs Nathan Sheets before the House Financial Services Subcommittee on Monetary Policy And Trade
sources.quote: In Greece, the IMF has been a key partner in Europe's efforts to support a difficult but necessary adjustment that encourages growth and puts debt on a more sustainable path. We stand behind the IMF Board's 2010 decision to provide the IMF with limited flexibility in its policy for exceptionally large financing.

stances.position_label: Optimistic about AI productivity, cautious on market valuations
stances.summary: Nathan Sheets has expressed cautious optimism about AI's economic potential, predicting a productivity boom comparable to the late 1990s internet era. However, he has also expressed concerns about market valuations, suggesting AI stocks are overvalued. He has not advocated for strong regulatory frameworks or safety requirements, focusing instead on economic impacts and market dynamics.

sources.title: Former Treasury official Nathan Sheets talks AI productivity, geopolitical tension in Duke address
sources.quote: Sheets predicted the technology will 'kick off a productivity boom in the United States that is at least as strong as what we had during the internet boom of the late '90s'

sources.title: Former Treasury official Nathan Sheets talks AI productivity, geopolitical tension in Duke address
sources.quote: Sheets commented that if he were the 'grand sultan of the world,' he would set the S&P 500 index 10% lower and AI stocks 15% lower

qualitative.summary: No court records, indictments, or ethics-violation findings were located for Nathan Sheets. His 2014 confirmation proceeded without reported opposition or ethics concerns.

sources.title: PN1455 - Nomination of D. Nathan Sheets for Department of the Treasury, 113th Congress (2013-2014)

qualitative.summary: Sheets filed standard OGE-278 financial-disclosure reports required for Senate-confirmed positions; no FOIA disputes or withholding orders were found.

sources.title: PN1455 - Nomination of D. Nathan Sheets for Department of the Treasury, 113th Congress (2013-2014)

qualitative.summary: He served 18 years at the Federal Reserve, including 4 as Director of International Finance, then as Under Secretary of the Treasury for International Affairs 2014-2017, confirmed unanimously.

sources.title: D. Nathan Sheets
D. Nathan Sheets

promise_record.promise: To secure approval for the 2010 IMF quota and governance reforms

promise_source.title: Treasury Undersecretary Nathan Sheets: Global Economy Falls Short of Aspirations
promise_source.quote: Treasury and the Obama administration 'are firmly committed to securing approval for the 2010 IMF quota and governance reforms'

promise_record.action: Continued advocacy for IMF reforms; reforms remained blocked by U.S. Congress through end of Obama administration and were only implemented in 2016 after congressional approval
promise_record.explanation: Sheets advocated consistently for IMF reforms but lacked unilateral authority to secure congressional approval; reforms passed after his tenure ended.

promise_record.promise: To bring China and other large emerging market countries into a new set of international export credit guidelines through the IWG

promise_source.title: Testimony of Under Secretary Nathan Sheets Before the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform
promise_source.quote: progress is being made, and achieving further progress will continue to be a top priority for Treasury and the Administration

promise_record.action: Continued IWG meetings through seventh session in February 2015 and planned eighth meeting; China remained engaged but comprehensive guidelines were not finalized during his tenure

action_sources.title: Testimony of Under Secretary Nathan Sheets Before the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

promise_record.explanation: Sheets sustained diplomatic engagement through IWG meetings but did not achieve final agreement on comprehensive guidelines before leaving office.

promise_record.promise: To make the interest rate provisions of the OECD Arrangement more market oriented

promise_source.title: Testimony of Under Secretary Nathan Sheets Before the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform
promise_source.quote: we have tabled a strong proposal in the OECD that, if adopted, would help to ensure that official export financing support does not substitute for private market finance

promise_record.action: Tabled formal proposal at OECD; continued advocacy for market-oriented reforms to Arrangement interest rate provisions

action_sources.title: Testimony of Under Secretary Nathan Sheets Before the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

promise_record.explanation: Sheets fulfilled procedural commitment by tabling the proposal, but adoption required multilateral agreement beyond his control.

promise_record.promise: To reduce intervention in foreign exchange markets as part of U.S.-China S&ED commitments

promise_source.title: Remarks by Under Secretary Nathan Sheets at the Center for Strategic & International Studies
promise_source.quote: China committed, for the first time, to reduce intervention in the foreign exchange market. China's intervention has in fact declined significantly since the last S&ED

promise_record.action: Documented and verified reduced Chinese foreign exchange intervention through 2015; continued monitoring and diplomatic pressure

action_sources.title: Remarks by Under Secretary Nathan Sheets at the Center for Strategic & International Studies

promise_record.explanation: Sheets accurately reported verified reduction in Chinese intervention and maintained diplomatic framework for continued pressure.

promise_record.promise: To advance bilateral investment treaty (BIT) negotiations with China covering all sectors with negative list approach

promise_source.title: Remarks by Under Secretary Nathan Sheets at the Center for Strategic & International Studies
promise_source.quote: The S&ED will continue to support the BIT negotiations and investment liberalization

promise_record.action: Continued S&ED support for BIT negotiations; exchanged negative lists with China in 2015 as per commitment

action_sources.title: Remarks by Under Secretary Nathan Sheets at the Center for Strategic & International Studies

promise_record.explanation: Sheels fulfilled commitment to sustain S&ED support for BIT negotiations, achieving concrete milestone of negative list exchange.

correlations.donor: Poiema Brands LLC d/b/a Honey Rock Group
correlations.position_or_vote: Market-oriented with government role in stability and level playing field
correlations.note: Sheets founded Nature Nate's Honey, a natural foods business, and campaign finance records show his own company entity made multiple in-kind contributions to his campaign; his stated economic philosophy emphasizes market-oriented approaches with government ensuring fair competition, which aligns with business interests in the natural foods sector.

sources.title: Nathan H Sheets - Texas Candidate - Transparency USA
Sheets Opens Fundraising Lead Over Miller in Texas Ag Commissioner Race

correlations.position_or_vote: Pro-free trade with rules-based framework; opposes unilateral protectionism
correlations.note: The Scharbauer family has extensive ranching and agricultural business interests in Texas; Sheets has stated he will pursue 'Texas First Trade' to benefit farmers and ranchers, while his documented position supports rules-based free trade frameworks, creating a correlation between agricultural exporter donor interests and trade policy positioning.

sources.title: Nathan H Sheets - Texas Candidate - Transparency USA
Nate Sheets on the Issues

correlations.position_or_vote: Optimistic about AI productivity, cautious on market valuations
correlations.note: Meta is a major technology and AI company; Sheets received substantial in-kind advertising services from Meta, and his documented position expresses optimism about AI productivity while maintaining caution on market valuations, showing alignment with technology sector interests in AI development.

sources.title: Nathan H Sheets - Texas Candidate - Transparency USA

correlations.donor: Harris Media LLC / Griffin Communications / Chabot Strategies LLC
correlations.position_or_vote: Market-oriented with government role in stability and level playing field
correlations.note: These political consulting and media firms received substantial payments for campaign services; the correlation exists between campaign infrastructure spending and Sheets' pro-business economic messaging, though these are operational expenditures rather than ideological donations.

sources.title: Nathan H Sheets - Texas Candidate - Transparency USA

correlations.position_or_vote: Active global leadership through international institutions
correlations.note: This religious radio program received the largest single expenditure; Sheets' campaign emphasizes 'America First' and 'Texas First' framing that contrasts with multilateral institutional approaches, creating tension rather than alignment with this donor's potential interests, though the expenditure was for advertising services.

sources.title: Nathan H Sheets - Texas Candidate - Transparency USA
Nate Sheets on the Issues

correlations.donor: John Knox Jr (Adoption Is an Option)
correlations.position_or_vote: Market-oriented with government role in stability and level playing field
correlations.note: Knox founded an adoption services organization and donated $50,000; Sheets' pro-life and adoption-focused platform aligns with this donor's organizational mission, representing a correlation between social issue advocacy and campaign support, though this falls outside the core economic policy positions documented.

sources.title: Nathan H Sheets - Texas Candidate - Transparency USA
Nate Sheets on the Issues

contradictions.description: The label describes market orientation with government role in stability, but the scalar 0.35 is closer to center than the description suggests; however, this is within acceptable range.

The scalar 0.3 suggests strong preference for employer flexibility, but the summary describes significant support for worker protections through unemployment benefits and demand-side stimulus, suggesting a more centrist position around 0.4-0.45.
